touchdesigner.definition: TouchDesigner is software for moving information around so it turns into images, sound, or interaction while it is happening.

signal flow.definition: Signal flow means information moves in a direction, step by step, from one place to the next.

operator.definition: An operator is one small part of the system that does one job and then passes the result along.

op taxonomy.definition: OP taxonomy is how TouchDesigner groups operators by what kind of job they do.

real time.definition: Real time means things update immediately while they are happening.

network.definition: A network is a group of connected parts that pass information to each other.

sop.definition: SOPs work with shapes in space.

dat.definition: DATs work with text and lists.

input.definition: An input is where information enters something.

output.definition: An output is where information leaves something.

viewer.definition: A viewer is a place where you can see what is happening.

parameter.definition: A parameter is a setting that changes how something behaves.

cook.definition: Cook means the system is doing its work right now.

time.definition: Time is what keeps everything moving forward.

data vs view.definition: Data is the information itself. View is how you look at that information.

state.definition: State means something remembers what happened before.

frame.definition: A frame is one update of the system.

rate.definition: Rate is how many frames happen over time.

context.definition: Context means where something exists and what it belongs to.

inside vs outside.definition: Inside means you are looking at something from within it. Outside means you are looking at it from the outside.

live vs stored.definition: Live means something keeps changing. Stored means something stays the same.

trigger / event.definition: A trigger or event is something that happens once when a condition is met.

channel.definition: Channel tells which MIDI channel the message is on.

units.definition: Units tell you what a number means.

palette.definition: The palette is a place where ready-made parts are listed so you can bring them into your work.

derivative.definition: Derivative is the main group of built-in parts made by the creators of the software.

generators.definition: Generators are parts that create something from nothing.

image filters.definition: Image filters change the look of images.

mapping.definition: Mapping is about fitting images onto shapes or spaces.

meta quest.definition: Meta Quest groups parts made to work with VR headsets.

pops.definition: POPs are parts that work with many small moving points.

td ableton.definition: TD Ableton groups parts that connect TouchDesigner to Ableton.

td bitwing.definition: TD Bitwing groups parts that connect TouchDesigner to Bitwing.

td synchro.definition: TD Synchro groups parts used to keep things in time together.

tdvr.definition: TDVR groups parts made for virtual reality setups.

tdvs.definition: TDVS groups parts made for video server workflows.

techniques.definition: Techniques groups parts that help manage how the system runs.

thread manager.definition: Thread Manager helps control how work is split up inside the system.

tools.definition: Tools group parts that help build, manage, or inspect things.

ui.definition: UI groups parts used to build interfaces people interact with.

vive.definition: Vive groups parts made to work with Vive hardware.

web rtc.definition: Web RTC groups parts for sending data over the internet in real time.

my components.definition: My Components is your personal collection of saved parts.

icon / info / suggestions.definition: This area shows details and hints about what is selected.

live 11.definition: Live 11 holds parts made to work with Ableton Live version 11.

live 9+10.definition: Live 9+10 holds parts made to work with older versions of Ableton Live.

extras.definition: Extras holds additional parts that are not required but can be useful.

system components.definition: System Components are parts that help the overall system work together.

utilities.definition: Utilities are small helper parts that do simple support jobs.

world objects.definition: World Objects are parts that exist inside a 3D space.

sick.definition: Sick holds example interface designs made to look polished.

basic widgets.definition: Basic Widgets are simple interface parts like buttons and sliders.

core.definition: Core holds the most basic and necessary parts.

gadgets.definition: Gadgets are small ready-made parts that do one clear thing.

package.definition: Package groups parts that are meant to be used together.

scripting.definition: Scripting groups parts that use written instructions to control behavior.

window placement.definition: Window Placement controls how the main window behaves and where it appears.

designer mode.definition: Designer Mode is for building and adjusting things.

performer mode.definition: Performer Mode is for showing the result without editing.

par.definition: Par means the window follows settings from a parameter.

net.definition: Net means the window placement is linked to the network.

open / close.definition: Open and Close control whether the window is visible or hidden.

path.definition: Path defines which part of the system the window is connected to.

custom position.definition: Custom Position lets you place the window exactly where you want.

midi mapper.definition: MIDI Mapper is where you connect MIDI controls to things in TouchDesigner.

device mappings.definition: Device Mappings is the list of all MIDI connections you have set up.

in device.definition: In Device is the MIDI device that sends messages into TouchDesigner.

out device.definition: Out Device is the MIDI device that TouchDesigner sends messages to.

midi map.definition: MIDI Map is the set of rules that say which MIDI message controls which thing.

ch.definition: Ch means which MIDI channel a message uses.

create new mapping.definition: Create New Mapping makes a new connection between a MIDI control and something in TouchDesigner.

check midi devices.definition: Check MIDI Devices looks for MIDI devices that are connected and available.

midi console.definition: MIDI Console shows the MIDI messages that are coming in or going out.

id.definition: ID is the label that helps tell one message apart from another.

type.definition: Type tells what kind of MIDI message it is.

index.definition: Index is the number that points to which control or part the message refers to.

value.definition: Value is the number carried by the message.

message.definition: Message is the full MIDI information being shown in the console.

console settings.definition: Console Settings control what the MIDI console shows.

enable logging.definition: Enable Logging turns message recording on or off.

in messages.definition: In Messages shows MIDI messages coming into TouchDesigner.

out messages.definition: Out Messages shows MIDI messages being sent out from TouchDesigner.

clock / timing messages.definition: Clock and Timing Messages show signals related to timing and sync.

sensing messages.definition: Sensing Messages show signals used to check if devices are still connected.

beat panel.definition: Beat Panel is where timing based on rhythm is handled.

time panel.definition: Time Panel shows timing information related to beats.

beat input.definition: Beat Input is where beat information comes from.

listen / tap.definition: Listen and Tap are ways to set a beat by listening or tapping.

tempo.definition: Tempo is how fast the beat is.

variables / macros / t-script.definition: This area is about names that stand in for values or text so they can be reused.

environment variables.definition: Environment variables are values set outside the project that the project can read.

built-in variables.definition: Built-in variables are values TouchDesigner already knows and provides automatically.

macros.definition: Macros are shortcuts that expand into longer text or values.

console.definition: The console is a place where messages from the system are shown.

textport.definition: Textport is a place where you type text commands and see text responses.

signal flow taxonomy.definition: Signal flow taxonomy is the system TouchDesigner uses to describe how information moves and connects.

one-to-one.definition: One-to-one means one thing feeds into one other thing.

one-to-many.definition: One-to-many means one thing feeds into multiple things.

many-to-one.definition: Many-to-one means multiple things feed into one thing.

chain.definition: A chain is a straight line of things connected one after another.

branch.definition: A branch is where one output feeds into multiple paths.

merge.definition: A merge is where multiple inputs come together into one.

pass-through.definition: Pass-through means information goes through unchanged.

process.definition: Process means information is changed as it moves through.

cook dependency.definition: Cook dependency means one thing updates because something before it changed.

upstream.definition: Upstream means earlier in the flow.

downstream.definition: Downstream means later in the flow.

family-specific flow rules.definition: Family-specific flow rules describe which kinds of data are allowed to connect to each other.

image data.definition: Image data is information that represents pictures.

value data.definition: Value data is information made of changing numbers.

shape data.definition: Shape data describes form and structure in space.

text data.definition: Text data is information written as words, lists, or tables.

surface data.definition: Surface data describes how something looks on the outside.

compatible.definition: Compatible means two things can connect directly.

incompatible.definition: Incompatible means two things cannot connect directly.

connection words.definition: Connection words are the terms TouchDesigner uses to describe how data is shared.

connect.definition: Connect means linking an output to an input.

reference.definition: Reference means pointing to something without copying it.

export.definition: Export means sending information out to be used elsewhere.

link.definition: Link means keeping two things connected so they stay in sync.

bind.definition: Bind means locking one thing to follow another.

fetch.definition: Fetch means pulling information from somewhere else.

pull vs push.definition: Pull means something updates because it is asked for. Push means something updates because it decides to send data out.

demand.definition: Demand means something only updates when something else needs it.

active vs inactive.definition: Active means something is currently being used. Inactive means it is not being used.

order of evaluation.definition: Order of evaluation is the order in which things are checked and updated.

side effects.definition: Side effects are changes that happen beyond the main result.

ownership.definition: Ownership means which part is responsible for the data.

scope.definition: Scope means how far something can be seen or used.

operator families.definition: Operator families are groups that tell you what kind of data an operator works with.

top family.definition: The TOP family works with images.

chop family.definition: The CHOP family works with changing numbers over time.

sop family.definition: The SOP family works with shapes and structure in space.

dat family.definition: The DAT family works with text, tables, and lists.

mat family.definition: The MAT family works with how surfaces look.

family compatibility.definition: Family compatibility means some families can connect directly and others cannot.

family boundaries.definition: Family boundaries explain where data must change form to keep moving.

shared mem in.definition: Shared Mem In receives data from another program using shared memory.

setup.definition: Setup contains the settings needed to send data to shared memory.

shared mem name.definition: Shared Mem Name is the name used so other programs know where to find the shared data.

objsharedmem.definition: objsharedmem is the default shared memory name used by TouchDesigner.

shared mem type.definition: Shared Mem Type describes what kind of data is being sent.

promote extension.definition: Promote Extension makes extension features visible at a higher level.

re-init extension.definition: Re-Init Extension restarts the extension logic.

viewer active.definition: Viewer Active controls whether the viewer is updating.

cook type.definition: Cook Type controls when the component updates.

node view.definition: Node View controls how the component looks in the network.

clone.definition: Clone allows the component to follow another one.

shared mem out.definition: Shared Mem Out sends data from TouchDesigner to another program using shared memory.

active.definition: Active controls whether data is being sent out or not.

ambient light.definition: Ambient Light adds overall light that affects everything evenly.

light.definition: Light contains settings that control the basic color and strength of the light.

light color.definition: Light Color sets the color of the ambient light.

dimmer.definition: Dimmer controls how strong the ambient light is.

render.definition: Render controls how things are drawn.

material render.definition: Material Render controls whether materials are used when drawing.

draw priority.definition: Draw Priority controls which things are drawn first or last.

pick priority.definition: Pick Priority controls which object is chosen when things overlap.

wireframe color.definition: Wireframe Color sets the color used for wireframe display.

extension.definition: Extension refers to added logic attached to the component.

object.definition: Object refers to the component itself as a controllable item.

re-init extensions.definition: Re-Init Extensions resets the extension logic.

init extensions on start.definition: Init Extensions on Start controls whether extensions start automatically.

parent shortcut.definition: Parent Shortcut is a quick way to refer to the parent component.

global op shortcut.definition: Global OP Shortcut is a name used to access this component globally.

internal op shortcut.definition: Internal OP Shortcut is a name used inside the component.

enable cloning.definition: Enable Cloning allows the component to follow another one.

clone master.definition: Clone Master is the component being followed.

load on demand.definition: Load on Demand means parts load only when needed.

enable external tox.definition: Enable External TOX allows the component to load from a file.

external tox path.definition: External TOX Path is where the external file is located.

reload custom parameters.definition: Reload Custom Parameters refreshes custom settings.

reload built-in parameters.definition: Reload Built-In Parameters refreshes default settings.

sub-component to load.definition: Sub-Component to Load selects which part to load from a file.

relative file path behavior.definition: Relative File Path Behavior controls how file paths are handled.

blend.definition: Blend combines multiple things into one result.

blend type.definition: Blend Type decides how things are combined.

sequence.definition: Sequence controls the order things are combined.

reset frame.definition: Reset Frame resets timing used by the blend.

weight.definition: Weight controls how much influence something has.

normal offset.definition: Normal Offset shifts blending along a surface direction.

orient.definition: Orient controls how direction is aligned.

axes.definition: Axes define directions used for orientation.

short rotation.definition: Short Rotation chooses the shortest turn direction.

xform.definition: Xform controls where the camera is, how it is turned, and its size scale.

transform order.definition: Transform Order sets the order that movement steps happen.

rotate order.definition: Rotate Order sets the order rotations are applied.

scale.definition: Scale changes the camera’s scale space.

pivot.definition: Pivot sets the point rotations happen around.

uniform scale.definition: Uniform Scale keeps scale equal in all directions.

parent transform source.definition: Parent Transform Source makes the camera follow another object.

look at.definition: Look At makes the camera face a target.

path op.definition: Path OP defines a path the camera can follow.

orient along path.definition: Orient Along Path makes direction follow the path.

pre-xform.definition: Pre-Xform applies movement before the main transform.

apply pre-transform.definition: Apply Pre-Transform turns the pre-transform on or off.

commit to main transform.definition: Commit to Main Transform makes the pre-transform permanent.

light mask.definition: Light Mask controls which lights affect this object.

bone.definition: A Bone controls movement and bending for connected parts, usually in a chain.

forward direction.definition: Forward Direction defines what direction counts as forward.

up vector.definition: Up Vector defines which direction is considered up.

roll position.definition: Roll Position controls rotation along the path.

orient up vector.definition: Orient Up Vector keeps orientation aligned upright.

auto bank factor.definition: Auto Bank Factor controls how much automatic tilt happens on turns.

bone settings.definition: Bone settings control bending and limits.

display link.definition: Display Link shows the bone connection.

rest angles.definition: Rest Angles define the default angle.

bone length.definition: Bone Length controls how long the bone is.

capture.definition: Capture controls how geometry is influenced by bones.

display capture geometry.definition: Display Capture Geometry shows the affected area.

camera.definition: Camera defines how the scene is viewed.

look at up vector.definition: Look At Up Vector defines what direction is considered up while looking.

view.definition: View controls how the camera sees the world.

projection.definition: Projection controls how 3D space is flattened into an image.

viewing angle method.definition: Viewing Angle Method chooses how viewing angles are calculated.

fov angle.definition: FOV Angle controls how wide the camera sees.

near.definition: Near sets how close objects can be before disappearing.

far.definition: Far sets how distant objects can be before disappearing.

window roll.definition: Window Roll rotates the camera view.

ipd shift.definition: IPD Shift adjusts eye separation for stereo viewing.

settings.definition: Settings control background and rendering behavior.

background color.definition: Background Color sets the color behind everything.

camera light mask.definition: Camera Light Mask controls which lights affect the camera view.

annotate.definition: A UI component used to label, comment on, or visually explain parts of a network.

window.definition: Controls an application window on your screen.

constraint.definition: An operator that limits or connects how things can move.

actor.definition: An operator that turns geometry into a physical object that can move, collide, and be affected by forces.

bullet solver.definition: The system that runs physics for actors.

force.definition: An operator that pushes, pulls, or spins physical objects.
